result.acceptanceStatus: Accepted: shared code surfaces cover the current known docs, chat, and mock requirements without recurring one-off wrappers, and all requested validation gates passed.
residualRisks: External host surfaces beyond the repo stories and mock previews should still be visually spot-checked for the new embedded and parchment frames.
result.summary: The shared code-surface family now directly covers metadata-heavy editors, embedded docs/chat usage, alternate framed diffs, empty states, and asymmetric before/after layouts. The Seraph diff wrapper was absorbed into reusable DiffViewer framing, chat/docs consumers now use shared file metadata and embedded density controls, and the direct component stories/tests cover the high-risk permutations called out in the state matrix.
